title: Review the uncommitted changes. Context: # Coder Handoff ## Task Canon v3.1 batch commit — 全修正済みファイルをコミットする ## Changed Files .gitignore assets/hooks/context-monitor.sh assets/scripts/canon-v3-sim-coder.sh assets/skills/agent-memory/experience.jsonl assets/skills/bookkeeping/experience.jsonl assets/s...
summary_text: - High: [freee-client.mjs](/Users/masayuki/Dev/claude-config/assets/skills/bookkeeping/scripts/freee-client.mjs#L189) only turns `401/403/429/5xx` into failures. Other API failures like `400/404/409/422` will be returned as if they were successful responses, and [bookkeeping-apply.mjs](/Users/masayuki/Dev/claude-config/assets/skills/bookkeeping/scripts/bookkeeping-apply.mjs#L75) plus [bookkeeping-apply.mjs](/Users/masayuki/Dev/claude-config/assets/skills/bookkeeping/scripts/bookkeeping-apply.mjs#L144) then mark update/upload actions as `applied` without validating the body. That can silently record failed freee writes as success. - High: [experience.jsonl](/Users/masayuki/Dev/claude-config/assets/skills/bookkeeping/experience.jsonl#L1), [experience.jsonl](/Users/masayuki/Dev/claude-config/assets/skills/fugue/experience.jsonl#L1), and [experience.jsonl](/Users/masayuki/Dev/claude-config/assets/skills/orchestra-delegator/experience.jsonl#L1) are raw session telemetry in version control. They include task summaries, internal workflow details, durations, and user/request text. That is a real security/privacy leak and also creates large, low-signal commit noise. - Medium: [context-monitor.sh](/Users/masayuki/Dev/claude-config/assets/hooks/context-monitor.sh#L17) doubles the checkpoint threshold from `30` to `60`, while [context-monitor.sh](/Users/masayuki/Dev/claude-config/assets/hooks/context-monitor.sh#L66) also downgrades the old forced `/handover` interrupt into a soft `/compact` suggestion. The file still describes itself as the `~85%` safeguard, so this weakens the existing context-loss protection materially. - Medium: [assets/skills/bookkeeping/scripts/.claude/.project-init-status](/Users/masayuki/Dev/claude-config/assets/skills/bookkeeping/scripts/.claude/.project-init-status#L2) and [assets/skills/notebooklm-shared/.fugue-managed-notebooklm](/Users/masayuki/Dev/claude-config/assets/skills/notebooklm-shared/.fugue-managed-notebooklm#L2) are machine-local runtime state. These counters, local paths, and install timestamps will churn across machines and sessions, but add no stable source value. Correctness: 4/10. Security: 3/10. Code quality: 5/10. ISSUES TO FIX: 1. Treat all freee API responses with `status_code >= 400` as failures in `freee-client.mjs`, or explicitly validate success in each `bookkeeping-apply.mjs` write path before marking actions `applied`. 2. Remove or sanitize the committed `experience.jsonl` telemetry files and add an ignore rule if these are meant to stay local. 3. Restore the context-monitor safety contract, or at minimum reconcile the threshold/message change with the documented `~85%` `/handover` behavior. 4. Stop versioning machine-local state files such as `.project-init-status*` and `.fugue-managed-notebooklm`, or normalize them to stable checked-in defaults. Assumption: I reviewed the tracked modified files from the handoff list; unrelated untracked files were not included in the score. `bash -n` and `node --check` passed on the changed executable scripts, so the problems above are semantic rather than syntax-level. SCORE: 4/10
